intended_use_full: Alzevita is intended for use by neurologists and radiologists experienced in the interpretation and analysis of brain MRI scans. It enables automated labelling, visualization, and volumetric measurement of the hippocampus from high-resolution T1-weighted MRI images. The software facilitates comparison of hippocampal volume against a normative dataset derived from MRI scans of healthy control subjects aged 55 to 90 years, acquired using standardized imaging protocols on 1.5T/3T MRI scanners.
target_condition: Neurodegenerative conditions affecting the hippocampus (e.g., Alzheimer's disease, mild cognitive impairment)
ground_truth: Consensus manual segmentation (STAPLE algorithm) derived from three certified radiologists using standardized segmentation protocols on 298 subjects from the ADNI dataset
other_metrics: Dice Score: 0.86 (95% CI: 0.85-0.86); Hausdorff Distance: 1.51 mm (95% CI: 1.43-1.59 mm); Correlation Coefficient: ≥0.82; Relative Volume Difference: ≤24.6%; Bland-Altman Mean Difference (Total Hippocampus Volume): ≤1010 mm³
